Summary: [BE] requireRider skips is_approved on 3 of 6 endpoints
Description: deliveryPartner.controller.ts:28-58 — requireRider only validates the session token exists/isn't expired. acceptOrder/acceptOffer individually check is_approved, but rejectOrder/markPickedUp/markDelivered don't — so a pending_verification rider signed up seconds ago can still call those three.

Summary: [BE] supabaseAdmin silently falls back to anon client
Description: config/database.ts:61-62 — export const supabaseAdmin = supabaseServiceKey ? createClient(...) : supabase; JWT-role validation logs loud errors on a bad key, but the fallback itself is unchanged: any DB operation meant to bypass RLS instead silently runs as anon and gets RLS-filtered, rather than failing closed. Given how many critical paths depend on supabaseAdmin (checkout, payments, admin), a misconfigured key degrades to confusing empty-result bugs instead of a loud startup failure.

Summary: [BE] storeOwner.routes.ts has no router-level auth
Description: Unlike every other role's router. deleteStoreProduct (103-151) looks up app_users by session token without filtering role='shopkeeper', unlike other handlers in the same file — inconsistent auth pattern, latent IDOR if stores.owner_id is ever set programmatically for a non-shopkeeper.

Summary: [BE] cancelOrder has no order-status guard
Description: database.service.ts:419-506 only blocks cancellation if a delivery partner is assigned — never checks if the order is already order_delivered. A delivered order can be cancelled and auto-refunded (payment_status === 'paid' triggers processRefund).

Summary: [BE] Admin updateOrderStatus has no state-machine check
Description: orders.controller.ts:205-265 — any admin session can move order_delivered back to pending_at_store, re-triggering customer notifications inappropriately.

Summary: [BE] Tracking endpoints leak PII cross-customer
Description: tracking.controller.ts — getOrderTracking, getOrderTrackingFull, getDriverLocationsForOrder, getTrackingHistory never check the requested orderId belongs to req.customerId, despite being requireCustomer-gated. Any logged-in customer can view any other customer's delivery address + rider phone/location by guessing/observing an order UUID.

Summary: [BE] Riders can spoof GPS / write arbitrary order status
Description: tracking.controller.ts:107-122 (updateAgentLocation) never checks agentId === req.riderId. addTrackingUpdate (64-87) accepts any status string with no ownership check and no enum validation, writing directly into customer_orders.status — despite a code comment explicitly saying this route 'must not be open.'

Summary: [BE] Delivery/pickup OTP is brute-forceable
Description: verifyDeliveryOTP (537-567), verifyPickupCode (862-934) — 4-digit codes, no rate limiting, no lockout, directly gate 'proof of delivery.'

Summary: [BE] Driver broadcast searches from delivery address, not store
Description: shopkeeper.controller.ts's broadcastToNearbyDrivers() (~638-646) and delivery.controller.ts:187-212 both select customer_orders.delivery_latitude/delivery_longitude as the search center. A driver 'nearby' the delivery address may be far from the store the order needs picking up from first, and vice versa — drivers close to the store but far from the eventual drop-off never get offered the order.

Summary: [Web] Razorpay amount fully client-computed, no recheck
Description: pages/CheckoutPage.tsx:427 -> services/paymentGateway.ts:76-89 posts a client-computed amount to /api/payment/create. Combined with backend order-total trust issues, there's no verified guardrail preventing a manipulated total from reaching payment creation.

Summary: [Web] Direct-from-browser order-write fallback path
Description: utils/apiBase.ts:18-21 (shouldUseBackendApi) + services/supabase.ts:483-750 (createOrder) — if VITE_API_URL is ever unset in production, the app falls back to writing customer_orders/store_orders/order_items directly from the browser via an anon-key client, letting the client set order_total/discount_amount/payment_status directly, relying entirely on unverified RLS.

Summary: [Web] Logout doesn't clear the cart
Description: context/AuthContext.tsx:166-186 (logoutUser) never calls clearCart(). Shared/kiosk device: User B logs in after User A logs out and sees/can check out User A's cart. LocationContext's saved location is likewise never cleared.

Summary: [Web] No global 401 handling on the customer site
Description: Only ShopkeeperApp.tsx/DriverApp.tsx check status === 401; the actual customer flow never detects an expired/revoked session, clears it, and redirects to login — user gets stuck with isAuthenticated: true but every API call silently failing.

Summary: [Web] useOrderTrackingRealtime.ts stale closure bug
Description: Lines 64-96,99-147 — the realtime-subscription effect's dependency array is [orderId, !!order] (a boolean), not [orderId, order], so the closure keeps referencing the first render's order object. Any order field not explicitly re-assigned in each update permanently reverts to its first-render value.

Summary: [Web] GSTIN/business name discarded from order payload
Description: pages/CheckoutPage.tsx:80-82 (state) / :451-474 (orderData — no gstin/businessName field). Business customers filling this in get no GST record on their invoice. Distinct from the mobile app's version, already fixed.

Summary: [Web] Invoice download sends raw UUID as Bearer token
Description: pages/OrdersPage.tsx:251-254 — Authorization: Bearer ${user.id}. Fragile/misleading auth pattern; would be directly exploitable if requireCustomer ever regressed to accepting bare UUIDs.

Summary: [Web] Realtime + unconditional 3s poll run simultaneously
Description: hooks/useOrderTrackingRealtime.ts:141 — setInterval(refreshOrderAndHistory, 3000) runs alongside the realtime channel with no health-gating, doubling backend load for no benefit when realtime is healthy.

Summary: [Web] No AbortController on in-flight tracking fetches
Description: useOrderTrackingRealtime.ts:64-96,154-165 — stale responses can land and overwrite state after unmount/navigation or after a newer request already resolved.

Summary: [Web] Driver location polled every 2s unconditionally
Description: useOrderTrackingRealtime.ts:151-165, no gating on realtime connection state — the website's own version of an already-documented customer-mobile-app bug.

Summary: [Admin] Admin login reads password_hash via anon-readable RLS
Description: services/adminAuthService.ts:85-104 (authenticateAdmin) calls getAdminClient() before any session exists, falling back to the anon-key client — then does bcrypt.compare() in the browser against a hash fetched over the public anon key. Anyone can dump every admin's password hash for offline cracking if the admins RLS policy still permits anon SELECT.

Summary: [Admin] getAdminClient() silently downgrades to anon client
Description: services/supabase.ts:46-55 — every function in adminAuthService.ts, adminService.ts, auditLog.ts, and every page calling getAdminClient() directly runs as fully anonymous the moment the session token is gone, with no error thrown.

Summary: [Admin] Admin creation has no server-side role check or RLS
Description: Confirmed via full grep of supabase/migrations/*.sql — zero RLS hits on admins. createAdmin() (adminAuthService.ts:190-227) does a plain insert with nothing at the DB layer stopping arbitrary role/permission assignment.

Summary: [Admin] Unchecked-mutation bug class in Notifications/AdminHeader
Description: NotificationsPage.tsx:221-239 (markAllRead/markOneRead/deleteNotification) and :76-82 (PushNotificationPanel.handleSend), plus AdminHeader.tsx:118-120 (markAllRead) — all call .update()/.delete()/.insert() against admin_notifications with no error or row-count check, then optimistically update local state regardless of success.

Summary: [Admin] Store approval is 'blind' — no verification docs shown
Description: StoresPage.tsx never selects/renders verification_document, verification_number, or owner_image_url — columns that exist and are already shown for delivery partners. Admin can click Approve without ever seeing the shopkeeper's submitted ID.

Summary: [Admin] PushNotificationPanel never actually sends a push
Description: NotificationsPage.tsx handleSend (~57-100) fetches Expo push tokens, then only inserts an admin_notifications log row and reports success — there is no actual Expo push API call anywhere in the function. Admins believe a broadcast went out when none did.

Summary: [Admin] No per-endpoint permission enforcement for admin roles
Description: hasPermission() (adminAuthService.ts:293-309) is only checked from React components; nothing at the RLS or backend-middleware layer enforces role. A viewer-role admin can bypass the UI to perform writes their role shouldn't allow.

Summary: [Admin] Password-strength schemas defined but never used
Description: admin/src/schemas/admin.schema.ts:11-39,41-68 enforce upper/lower/digit/special-char, but CreateAdminPage.tsx:65 / EditAdminPage.tsx:110 only check length < 8 — weak 8-character passwords like 'aaaaaaaa' are accepted for admin accounts.

Summary: [DB] Migrations can't bootstrap schema from an empty DB
Description: Grepping every CREATE TABLE for coupons, app_users, stores, admins, categories, master_products, order_items, store_orders, and 12 other tables returns zero matches for all of them — only delivery_partners has a real tracked CREATE TABLE. supabase db reset or a fresh project from these migrations alone would fail immediately.

Summary: [DB] customer_orders.payment_method type never created
Description: 20260404120000_customer_orders_razorpay_payment_id.sql:109 declares type public.payment_method_type, but the enum actually created later is named public.payment_method (no _type suffix). Replayed purely from tracked migrations, this fails.

Summary: [DB] public.order_status enum has no CREATE TYPE
Description: The single most important enum in the app (drives all order lifecycle transitions) — used from 20260404120000 onward and later ALTER TYPE ... ADD VALUE'd, but never created.

Summary: [DB] SECURITY DEFINER functions missing SET search_path
Description: accept_driver_offer(), auto_offline_stale_drivers(), is_account_locked(), is_admin_authenticated(), notify_admin_new_order(), notify_admin_new_user(), debug_admin_headers(). is_admin_authenticated() is the most sensitive — sole gate for every admin_full_access RLS policy across 9 tables. Standard Postgres privilege-escalation hardening gap.

Summary: [DB] debug_admin_headers() diagnostic function never dropped
Description: Despite its own comment saying 'safe to drop once resolved' (it has been). SECURITY DEFINER, no search_path pin, granted to anon/authenticated, returns the full matching admin_sessions row (including session_token) to any caller with a valid x-admin-token.

Summary: [Store] Push notifications never initialized at app startup
Description: lib/notifications.ts's notificationService.initialize() has no caller anywhere in the app — the only way a token gets registered is manually opening Settings -> Notifications -> Enable, despite copy implying it's on by default.

Summary: [Store] No global 401 handling
Description: lib/api-client.ts's request() never inspects 401s to trigger logout; error-handler.ts has a handleAuthError() that's dead code. Every screen shows empty data indefinitely on an expired session.

Summary: [Store] clearSession() doesn't clear the store-list cache
Description: session.ts:56-63 never calls clearStoreCache() — real cross-account data leak window on shared devices; shopkeeper B briefly sees A's store name/address/approval status.

Summary: [Store] Push token never cleared on logout
Description: Same root cause as the initialize() gap — a shared device could keep delivering one shopkeeper's order notifications to whoever's currently logged in.

Summary: [Store] useRequireStoreApproval() is reactive, not preventive
Description: The redirect only fires after an async round-trip resolves; Orders/Payouts/Inventory data-fetching effects run in parallel unconditioned on the approval check. If the request errors, the guard fails open silently forever.

Summary: [Store] Orphaned inventory.tsx route has no approval guard
Description: Still deep-link-reachable via Expo Router's file-based routing even though no in-app navigation targets it; writes directly to the DB.

Summary: [Rider] Service-role key plumbed into the client bundle
Description: constants/config.ts:33-37 + app.config.js:86-90 — if EXPO_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY is ever set at build time, the full-privilege key ships inside every installed APK/IPA, extractable via decompilation.

Summary: [Rider] Unauthenticated fallback leaks every rider's PII
Description: app/(tabs)/profile.tsx:110-135 — if /delivery-partner/profile fails, falls back to fetching the entire delivery-partner roster with no token, exposing every other rider's verification documents, phone, and address.

Summary: [Rider] 'Always' location requested but never used
Description: app.config.js configures always-permission, but code only calls requestForegroundPermissionsAsync() and uses foreground-only watchPositionAsync + a JS setInterval heartbeat. A rider mid-delivery with a locked phone has stale location while still being dispatched new offers.

Summary: [Rider] Reinstatement invisible until app restart
Description: The status-approval poll only starts when driverStatus === 'pending_verification' — a rider reinstated while the app stays open has no way to discover it short of force-quitting.

Summary: [Rider] Approval detection is poll-only, no realtime
Description: Unlike order offers, which do use Realtime. A newly-signed-up rider waits up to 30s after admin approval to see it reflected.

Summary: [Rider] No ErrorBoundary anywhere in the app
Description: An unhandled render exception crashes the whole app with no fallback UI.

Summary: [Cust] Logout doesn't clear cart/push token
Description: One of 6 previously-known issues, still open.

Summary: [Cust] Service-role key still required for 4 files
Description: lib/supabase.ts:31-54 builds an admin client from a service-role key; addressService.ts, authService.ts, product/coupons.tsx, distanceUtils.ts all still depend on it. Either the key ships inside every installed app (full DB compromise) or these features are broken in production. Needs an architecture fix routing through the authenticated backend. (Order placement's piece of this is already fixed.)

Summary: [Cust] IDOR on authService.ts/addressService.ts
Description: Both take a bare userId string with zero check it matches the authenticated session. Anyone can query any other customer's full profile and saved addresses by ID alone. (Order placement's piece of this is already fixed; read-only order-history/status lookups also still open.)

Summary: [Cust] Cart is never cleared after a successful order
Description: COD or paid — the only clearCart() call site is in the failed/cancelled payment branch. A customer can accidentally re-order the same items.

Summary: [Cust] No cart quantity ceiling or stock check anywhere
Description: CartContext.tsx places no upper bound on quantity, and createOrder never validates against any inventory field before inserting.

Summary: [Cust] discount_amount hardcoded to 0 on every order
Description: orderService.ts:375 — regardless of an applied coupon; the discount is folded into total_amount, but the dedicated finance/reporting column is always wrong.

Summary: [Cust] Coupon expiry is fetched but never enforced
Description: Client or server side — an expired-but-still-active coupon remains applicable with zero re-validation at order-creation time.

Summary: [Cust] Google Maps key called directly, bypassing backend proxy
Description: Confirmed direct calls to maps.googleapis.com at multiple screens; backend/src/routes/places.routes.ts already provides an equivalent proxy and is never called. Ships a billable, extractable key inside every installed app.

Summary: [Cust] Invoice screen is fabricated client-side; PDF is a stub
Description: app/order/invoice/[id].tsx:38-54 builds a fake invoice locally, never calling the real backend invoice endpoint. Download shows a 'coming in next update' alert. Customers cannot get a real tax invoice from the app.
